hypothesis: If a cross-modal predictive architecture jointly trains graph (CGCNN), text (MatSciBERT-encoded RoboCrystallographer descriptions), and simulated XRD (1D CNN) modalities using latent-space cross-modal prediction losses with VIC regularization and contrastive loss, then it will achieve lower mean absolute error on Matbench crystal property tasks and AFLOW band-gap prediction than graph-only baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) while requiring minimal parameter updates during finetuning because complementary structural information and cross-modal alignment produce richer, more generalizable embeddings.
